cluster_ids.name: On/Off
cluster_ids.summary: The On/Off cluster provides basic functionality to control the on/off state of a device. It supports features like Lighting, DeadFrontBehavior, and OffOnly.

classifications.summary: This cluster is a base application cluster operating at the endpoint level, providing core on/off control functionality.

revisions.description: Mandatory global ClusterRevision attribute added; CCB 1555
FeatureMap global attribute support with Level Control and Lighting feature
New data model format and notation
Addition of Dead Front behavior and associated FeatureMap entry
Addition of OffOnly feature and associated FeatureMap entry

features.name: Lighting
features.summary: Supports lighting applications. On Level Control command setting OnOff to FALSE, `OnTime` SHALL be 0. On Level Control command setting OnOff to TRUE, if `OnTime` is 0, `OffWaitTime` SHALL be 0.

features.name: DeadFrontBehavior
features.summary: Device exhibits 'dead front' behavior when `OnOff` is FALSE. Other clusters MAY fail Invoke/Write with `INVALID_IN_STATE` or report best-effort attribute values. Starts on `OnOff` TRUE->FALSE, stops on FALSE->TRUE. Attribute changes due to dead front SHALL be processed.

features.name: OffOnly
features.summary: Device supports `Off` command, but `On` and `Toggle` commands SHALL NOT be supported. Useful for devices that can only be turned off via commands due to regulatory requirements.
features.dependencies: if neither LT nor DF are supported

values.name: AcceptOnlyWhenOn
values.summary: Indicates a command is only accepted when in On state.

values.name: Off
values.summary: Set the OnOff attribute to FALSE

values.name: On
values.summary: Set the OnOff attribute to TRUE

values.name: Toggle
values.summary: If the previous value of the OnOff attribute is equal to FALSE, set the OnOff attribute to TRUE. If the previous value of the OnOff attribute is equal to TRUE, set the OnOff attribute to FALSE (toggle).

values.name: DelayedOffSlowFade
values.summary: 50% dim down in 0.8 seconds then fade to off in 12 seconds

values.name: DyingLightFadeOff
values.summary: 20% dim up in 0.5s then fade to off in 1 second

attributes.name: OnOff
attributes.summary: Indicates whether the device type implemented on the endpoint is turned off (FALSE) or turned on (TRUE). If the Scenes Management server cluster is implemented, this attribute SHALL be part of the ExtensionFieldSetStruct of the Scene Table.

attributes.name: GlobalSceneControl
attributes.summary: Controls global scene behavior. Set to TRUE when OnOff becomes TRUE (e.g., via On, MoveToLevel(WithOnOff), RecallScene, OnWithRecallGlobalScene). Set to FALSE after OffWithEffect. Prevents storing all-off or destroying settings by recalling global scene.

attributes.name: OnTime
attributes.summary: Specifies the length of time (in 1/10ths second) that the On state SHALL be maintained before automatically transitioning to the Off state when using the OnWithTimedOff command. Writable at any time, but takes effect only when in the Timed On state.

attributes.name: OffWaitTime
attributes.summary: Specifies the length of time (in 1/10ths second) that the Off state SHALL be guarded to prevent another OnWithTimedOff command turning the server back to its On state. Writable at any time, but takes effect when transitioning to or in the Delayed Off state.

attributes.name: StartUpOnOff
attributes.summary: Defines the desired startup behavior of a device when supplied with power, reflected in the OnOff attribute. If null, OnOff is set to its previous value. Otherwise, behavior is defined by StartUpOnOffEnum. Does not apply to OTA reboots.

commands.name: Off
commands.summary: Turns the device off. If the OnTime attribute is supported, it is reset to 0.
commands.effect_on_receipt:
set OnOff attribute := FALSE
IF OnTime attribute IS supported THEN
  set OnTime attribute := 0
END IF

commands.name: On
commands.summary: Turns the device on. If the OffOnly feature is supported, the command is rejected. Resets OffWaitTime if OnTime is 0 and both are supported.
commands.effect_on_receipt:
IF OffOnly feature IS supported THEN
  send UNSUPPORTED_COMMAND failure status response
ELSE
  set OnOff attribute := TRUE
END IF
IF OnTime attribute IS supported AND OffWaitTime attribute IS supported THEN
  IF OnTime attribute value == 0 THEN
    set OffWaitTime attribute := 0
  END IF
END IF

commands.name: Toggle
commands.summary: Toggles the device's on/off state. If the OffOnly feature is supported, the command is rejected. Adjusts OnTime or OffWaitTime based on the new state.
commands.effect_on_receipt:
IF OffOnly feature IS supported THEN
  send UNSUPPORTED_COMMAND failure status response
ELSE
  IF OnOff attribute value == FALSE THEN
    set OnOff attribute := TRUE
  ELSE
    set OnOff attribute := FALSE
  END IF
END IF
IF OnTime attribute IS supported AND OffWaitTime attribute IS supported THEN
  IF OnOff attribute value == FALSE AND OnTime attribute value == 0 THEN
    set OffWaitTime attribute := 0
  END IF
  IF OnOff attribute value == TRUE THEN
    set OnTime attribute := 0
  END IF
END IF

commands.name: OffWithEffect
commands.summary: Allows devices to be turned off using enhanced fading effects. Stores current settings in a global scene if GlobalSceneControl is TRUE before turning off.

fields.name: EffectIdentifier
fields.summary: Specifies the fading effect to use when turning the device off. SHALL contain one of the non-reserved values listed in EffectIdentifierEnum.

fields.name: EffectVariant
fields.summary: Indicates which variant of the effect (from EffectIdentifier) SHOULD be triggered. If not supported, the default variant is used. Dependent on EffectIdentifier, values from DelayedAllOffEffectVariantEnum or DyingLightEffectVariantEnum.

commands.effect_on_receipt:
check GlobalSceneControl attribute value
IF GlobalSceneControl attribute value == TRUE THEN
  store current settings in global scene
  set GlobalSceneControl attribute := FALSE
  set OnOff attribute := FALSE
  IF OnTime attribute IS supported THEN
    set OnTime attribute := 0
  END IF
ELSE IF GlobalSceneControl attribute value == FALSE THEN
  set OnOff attribute := FALSE
END IF

commands.name: OnWithRecallGlobalScene
commands.summary: Recalls the settings from the global scene when the device was turned off. Discards the command if GlobalSceneControl is TRUE.
commands.effect_on_receipt:
IF GlobalSceneControl attribute value == TRUE THEN
  discard command
ELSE IF GlobalSceneControl attribute value == FALSE THEN
  call Scenes Management cluster server.recall_global_scene()
  update OnOff attribute based on recalled scene
  set GlobalSceneControl attribute := TRUE
END IF
IF OnTime attribute IS supported AND OffWaitTime attribute IS supported THEN
  IF OnTime attribute value == 0 THEN
    set OffWaitTime attribute := 0
  END IF
END IF

commands.name: OnWithTimedOff
commands.summary: Turns devices on for a specific duration with a guarded off duration. Prevents subsequent OnWithTimedOff commands from turning devices back on during the guarded off period. Updates the on period if received while already on.
commands.timing: Attributes are updated every 1/10th second if not 0xFFFF until both OnTime and OffWaitTime are 0.

fields.name: OnOffControl
fields.summary:
Contains information on how the server is to be operated. Includes the AcceptOnlyWhenOn bit:
IF OnOffControl.AcceptOnlyWhenOn bit == 1 THEN command_accepted_only_if_OnOff_is_TRUE
ELSE command_processed_unconditionally

fields.name: OnTime
fields.summary: Used to adjust the value of the OnTime attribute.

fields.name: OffWaitTime
fields.summary: Used to adjust the value of the OffWaitTime attribute.

commands.effect_on_receipt:
IF OnOffControl.AcceptOnlyWhenOn == 1 AND OnOff attribute value == FALSE THEN
  discard command
ELSE IF OffWaitTime attribute value > 0 AND OnOff attribute value == FALSE THEN
  set OffWaitTime attribute := MIN(OffWaitTime attribute value, OffWaitTime field value)
ELSE
  set OnTime attribute := MAX(OnTime attribute value, OnTime field value)
  set OffWaitTime attribute := OffWaitTime field value
  set OnOff attribute := TRUE
END IF

IF OnTime attribute value != 0xFFFF AND OffWaitTime attribute value != 0xFFFF THEN
  start_periodic_timer(interval: 1/10 second)
  ON timer_tick DO
    IF OnOff attribute value == TRUE AND OnTime attribute value > 0 THEN
      decrement OnTime attribute by 1
      IF OnTime attribute value == 0 THEN
        set OffWaitTime attribute := 0
        set OnOff attribute := FALSE
      END IF
    ELSE IF OnOff attribute value == FALSE AND OffWaitTime attribute value > 0 THEN
      decrement OffWaitTime attribute by 1
      IF OffWaitTime attribute value == 0 THEN
        stop_periodic_timer()
      END IF
    END IF
  END ON
END IF

State Description.behavior: OnTime is decremented every 1/10s, unless its value equals 0xFFFF.
State Description.pseudocode: if OnTime != 0xFFFF then every 100ms: OnTime := OnTime - 1

State Description.behavior: OffWaitTime is decremented every 1/10s, unless its value equals 0xFFFF.
State Description.pseudocode: if OffWaitTime != 0xFFFF then every 100ms: OffWaitTime := OffWaitTime - 1

references.name: FeatureMap
references.description: A bitmap attribute used to indicate which optional features a cluster supports. Not fully defined in this section.

references.name: Level Control cluster
references.description: Another Matter cluster mentioned as a source of commands that can affect the On/Off cluster's attributes. Not defined in this section.

references.name: OnTime attribute
references.description: An attribute within the On/Off cluster whose value is set to 0 on receipt of a Level Control cluster command that causes the OnOff attribute to be set to FALSE. Not fully defined in this section.

references.name: OffWaitTime attribute
references.description: An attribute within the On/Off cluster that is set to 0 if the OnOff attribute is set to TRUE by a Level Control command and OnTime is 0. Not fully defined in this section.

references.name: OnOff attribute
references.description: The primary attribute of this cluster, representing the on/off state of the device. Its behavior is described, but its full definition (ID, type, etc.) is not provided here.

references.name: INVALID_IN_STATE status code
references.description: A status code used for failure responses when a device exhibits 'dead front' behavior. Not defined in this section.

references.name: ClusterRevision
references.description: Global attribute for cluster version, its value SHALL be the highest revision number in the table. Not defined in this section.

references.name: ZLO 1.0
references.description: Zigbee Light Link (ZLL) Profile 1.0, an external specification referenced for StartUpOnOff behavior. Not defined here.

references.name: CCB 1555
references.description: Change Control Board issue referenced in Revision 1. Not defined here.

references.name: Off Command
references.description: A command (ID 0x00) of the On/Off cluster used to turn the device off.

references.name: On Command
references.description: A command (ID 0x01) of the On/Off cluster used to turn the device on.

references.name: Toggle Command
references.description: A command (ID 0x02) of the On/Off cluster used to toggle the device's on/off state.

references.name: Data Model Specification
references.description: Base types (external spec) such as map8, enum8, bool, uint16.

references.name: Scenes Management server cluster
references.description: A server cluster that, if implemented on the same endpoint, uses the OnOff attribute as part of its ExtensionFieldSetStruct.

references.name: ExtensionFieldSetStruct
references.description: A structure within the Scenes Management server cluster that includes the OnOff attribute.

references.name: Scenes Cluster
references.description: A server cluster that manages scene storage. The OnOff attribute can be part of its ExtensionFieldSetStruct, and RecallScene command (from this cluster) can influence GlobalSceneControl.

references.name: MoveToLevel(WithOnOff) command
references.description: A command (likely from the Level Control Cluster) that can cause the OnOff attribute to be set to TRUE, influencing the GlobalSceneControl attribute.

references.name: StartUpOnOffEnum
references.description: An enumeration that defines the desired startup behavior of a device when it is supplied with power, influencing the initial state of the OnOff attribute.

references.name: EffectIdentifierEnum
references.description: Enumeration of fading effects for OffWithEffect command (not defined in this section).

references.name: DelayedAllOffEffectVariantEnum
references.description: Enumeration of variants for delayed all off effect (not defined in this section).

references.name: DyingLightEffectVariantEnum
references.description: Enumeration of variants for dying light effect (not defined in this section).

references.name: Scenes Management cluster
references.description: A Matter cluster responsible for managing and recalling scenes (interacted with by OnWithRecallGlobalScene command).

references.name: OnOffControlBitmap
references.description: Bitmap field type for OnOffControl field in OnWithTimedOff command (not defined in this section).

references.name: On/Off Cluster Operation State Machine
references.description: A diagram illustrating the operation of the On/Off cluster with respect to On, Off, and OnWithTimedOff commands (mentioned in 1.5.8 State Description).
